metadata.title: The CLARITY Act fight (H.R. 3633) — crypto market-structure legislation vs the illicit-finance / AML coalition (both sides graded)
metadata.status: the bill's status, Warren's AML framework, and the illicit-finance-risk pattern are FACT (Congress.gov/Senate Banking/reporting); the specific Jun-23-2026 Catholic-coalition letter is USER-REPORTED from X (not independently fetched — broader religious+AML opposition pattern IS corroborated); each side graded reasonable<->overreach; composition guard (map coalitions' ACTIONS, not 'feelings')
metadata.frame: The legislative endgame of the crypto-enforcement pendulum. The Digital Asset Market Clarity Act (H.R. 3633) would split digital-asset jurisdiction between the SEC and CFTC (most tokens -> CFTC 'digital commodities'), curb the Fed on CBDC, and give the industry the market-structure certainty it sought after losing in court and buying influence (Fairshake). It passed the House, cleared Senate Banking 15-9 (14 May 2026), and sits on the Senate calendar (1 Jun 2026) — but faces a 60-vote floor, reconciliation with the Ag Committee + House versions, an ETHICS-PROVISION fight over officials profiting from crypto (the Trump/World Liberty Financial conflict), and an AML/illicit-finance OPPOSITION coalition (Sen. Warren + Treasury-aligned, law-enforcement, anti-trafficking, and religious organizations). The opposition's concrete referent is the documented crypto trafficking-finance the corpus already maps (SE-Asia scam compounds, Huione, USDT). Ties to spec-crypto-legislation-forcing, spec-crypto-enforcement-actors, spec-crypto-market-makers-political (Fairshake), spec-se-asia-scam-crime-nexus, influence-operator-network (WLF).

the_bill: H.R. 3633, the Digital Asset Market Clarity Act ('CLARITY Act'), 119th Congress: establishes SEC/CFTC regulation of digital-commodity offer/sale (shifting most tokens to the CFTC as 'digital commodities'), bars Federal Reserve banks from offering retail products and from a monetary-policy CBDC, and provides the market-structure framework the industry wanted. Status: House-passed; Senate Banking advanced it 15-9 (14 May 2026, all 13 Republicans + 2 Democrats); placed on the Senate Legislative Calendar (Calendar No. 423, 1 Jun 2026). Remaining hurdles: reconciliation with the Senate Ag version + the House version, a 60-vote floor vote, and an unresolved ethics provision. grade: fact.
the_pro_side: The industry case (graded reasonable-in-part): years of 'regulation by enforcement' without clear rules (the Gensler era) left builders unable to comply; courts partially agreed (Ripple, Grayscale); clear SEC/CFTC lines reduce that uncertainty. The industry also spent to get here — Fairshake's ~$193M war chest + the operator-network now holding office (spec-crypto-market-makers-political, influence-operator-network). The pro-crypto framing of the opposition (per the user-supplied X reply) is that 'incumbents' are weaponizing illicit-finance fear — even 'manipulating religious organizations' — to throw out innovation with the bathwater. grade: the rules-certainty case is reasonable; the 'manipulation' characterization is contested opinion.
the_opposition: The AML/illicit-finance case (graded reasonable-in-part): Sen. Warren's framework demands the bill keep the FULL suite of Treasury-requested AML/CFT tools, close DeFi/intermediary loopholes, close sanctions loopholes, and bar officials (e.g., Trump) from profiting from crypto. A coalition of law-enforcement, anti-corruption, anti-trafficking, and (per the Jun-23-2026 letter) Catholic organizations warned Senate leadership the bill 'could weaken safeguards against illicit finance and create vulnerabilities that traffickers and transnational criminal organizations may exploit.' The concrete referent is real and already in this corpus: the SE-Asia pig-butchering compounds, the Huione/Tudou marketplaces, and USDT-rail laundering. grade: the illicit-finance risk is documented/reasonable; whether THIS bill widens it vs. the status quo is contested (the bill's defenders say it adds, not removes, AML).
the_ethics_provision: The hardest knot: the bill's progress is gated on an ETHICS provision addressing government officials' ties to the crypto industry — pointed squarely at the Trump-family World Liberty Financial / USD1 venture (influence-operator-network). Warren + Reed pressed DOJ/Treasury on WLF token sales to illicit actors. So the same conflict-of-position the corpus flags (the operator-network writing the rules for its own industry) is the explicit blocker on the Senate floor. grade: fact (the provision fight; the WLF pressure letters).
the_honest_reading: This is the crypto-enforcement PENDULUM reaching the legislature: after the courts and the 2025 SEC reversal, the industry (funded by Fairshake, staffed into office via the operator-network) is close to codifying favorable market structure — while an AML/anti-trafficking/religious coalition (Warren-aligned) invokes the very illicit-finance harms this corpus maps (SE-Asia scams, Huione, USDT) to demand stronger guardrails, and the Trump/WLF self-dealing conflict stalls the floor. BOTH the rules-certainty case AND the illicit-finance case are partly reasonable; the 'manipulating religious groups' and 'this bill guts AML' claims are each contested. Composition guard: the 'Catholic organizations,' 'law enforcement,' and 'the industry' are coalitions taking documented POSITIONS — not unitary minds with feelings. Overlay; excluded from the proofs.

edges.raw_instrument: crypto market-structure bill (H.R. 3633); House-passed; Senate Banking 15-9 (May 2026); calendar #423; needs 60-vote floor + reconciliation
splits jurisdiction — narrows SEC reach over 'digital commodities'
makes the CFTC primary regulator of most digital commodities
leads AML/ethics opposition; 5-priority framework (full Treasury AML tools, close DeFi/sanctions loopholes, bar officials profiting)
Jun-23-2026 letter to Senate leadership: bill 'could weaken safeguards against illicit finance' exploitable by traffickers/TCOs (user-reported; broader religious+AML opposition corroborated)
argues weak AML/CFT + DeFi-intermediary exemptions widen the illicit-finance gap
the concrete illicit-finance referent — pig-butchering trafficking-finance the opposition invokes
USDT the dominant illicit-finance settlement rail
Warren/Reed pressed DOJ/Treasury on WLF token sales to illicit actors — the ethics-provision sticking point
